_comment: Hub bridge engine conformance (wire 0.4). Drives the shared `_shared/stream_utils.run_bridge` (Python) and `_shared/stream_utils.runBridge` (Node) with a minimal identity parse_event that maps {partial/result/error} (+ optional session_id) into EventResult fields, feeding each scenario's `cli_stdout` lines as the wrapped CLI's NDJSON output. Both implementations MUST produce identical `bridge_stdout` lines and the listed `exit_code`. Add scenarios here when stream_utils.py and stream_utils.js drift on: error-mid-stream preserving session_id on the error event, empty-message+attachment accepted, FileNotFoundError → emit_error, last-wins ordering, session stamped on partials when known early. Per-CLI parse_event variations are covered in each hub's own tests; this fixture covers the shared engine.

scenarios.name: error mid-stream preserves session id
scenarios._note: Spec: session MUST persist on error-terminated turns. session_id is stamped on the error event (not a separate session event) so the next turn can resume.

scenarios.name: session discovered at end (typical CLI-wrapping case)
scenarios._note: Many CLIs only learn their session id at exit. A result event with session_id but no text key means 'session only' — the bridge falls back to the last partial as the reply body for non-streaming consumers.

scenarios.name: empty message + attachment is accepted (image-only turn)
empty message and no attachment → bridge emits error and exits 1
scenarios._note: Both bridges emit a `turn.message is required` error — the wording MAY differ slightly per language; `stdout_lines_any_of` accepts either the canonical form or the plain-cli variant (which prefixes the CLI name).

scenarios.name: CLI exits non-zero without session and without error event → bridge surfaces exit + stderr
scenarios._note: The bridges produce a single {"type":"error",...} line whose message embeds both the exit code and stderr snippet. Wording varies per language; we assert substrings rather than exact match.

scenarios.name: CLI exits non-zero but session was captured → session still persisted
scenarios._note: When the CLI exits non-zero but the bridge captured a session id and some reply text, the bridge still emits result and exits 0. Non-zero exit without a session is what surfaces as an error.

scenarios.name: final text falls back to last partial when no text on result
scenarios._note: When the CLI streams only via partials and the terminal result carries session_id without a text key, the bridge uses the last partial text as the final reply body so a non-streaming consumer still gets the answer.
